Rule: Start AndX scan immediately after SMB header
Notes: The parser uses `sizeof(struct smb_hdr) - 1` to locate the first AndX block.

Rule: Current AndX block must stay within packet bounds
Notes: Malformed chains that point outside the packet are rejected.

Rule: Next AndX offset must point within packet
Notes: Protects the dispatcher from arbitrary offset jumps.

Rule: Maximum chain depth is capped at 32
Notes: This is an implementation safety bound rather than a protocol-advertised capability.

Rule: Response sub-buffer must fit within allocated response area
AndXOffset is set to current PDU length when chaining
AndX command is propagated when client requested chaining
Notes: Common chain target after session setup.

Rule: Pipe read supports chained follow-on commands
Notes: RPC-over-pipe clients can depend on this path.

Rule: Write response supports chained follow-on commands
Notes: Needs targeted interop with legacy clients.

Rule: Close response does not emit further AndX by default

Notes: One of the few explicit fixed-format AndX responses.
